---
title: "Platform Vision: From Tool to Artist's Studio"
subtitle: "The evolution of SPARMVET_VIZ from a pipeline to a user-empowering AMR insight platform"
date: last-modified
---

## 1. The Shift: Tool Building → Platform Visioning

SPARMVET_VIZ began as an engineering challenge: build a robust, reproducible pipeline for processing multi-source data (eg. AMR surveillance). By the end of Phase 9, that challenge has been solved. The pipeline (metadata → phenotypes → genotypes → tidy frame) is verified, stable, and modular.

The next chapter asks a different question: **how does a scientist actually use this system?**

The answer is the **Platform Vision**: transform the internal tool into an accessible, self-serve analytical studio — where a veterinary epidemiologist with no Python experience can produce publication-quality, domain-specific AMR visualisations by editing a YAML file.

---

## 2. The User-as-Artist Philosophy

> *"The artist does not describe the paint — the artist describes the painting."*

The `VizFactory (viz_factory.py)` was deliberately designed around this principle. The user never writes Plotnine code. Instead, they write a **manifest** — a human-readable YAML recipe that describes *what* they want to see. The factory translates intent into pixels.

Three properties make this possible:

| Property | Mechanism | Effect |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Declarative Recipes** | YAML manifest with `layers:` list | User composes, not programs |
| **Sensible Defaults** | ADR-024 injection policy | Minimal manifests produce coherent plots |
| **Copy-Modify-Render** | Gallery & Recipe Pattern (ADR-025) | Self-service customisation |

This approach directly supports **Low-Level Coding** — the practice where domain scientists interact with code at the YAML abstraction layer, not the Python layer. The YAML manifest *is* the code.

---

## 3. The Gallery & Recipe Pattern (ADR-025)

The Gallery is a **Visual Cookbook** — a curated collection of verified plot recipes. Each entry is a 3-part **Recipe**:

::: {.callout-important}
**The 3-Part Recipe Contract (ADR-025)**

1. **Data Header** — A TSV snippet (first 5 rows) showing the expected column structure.
2. **YAML Manifest** — The exact copy-paste manifest that produced the plot. Nothing more, nothing less.
3. **Rendered Plot** — The PNG output from that exact manifest, serving as visual confirmation.
:::

This pattern ensures that **every plot in the system is fully reproducible by copying its recipe**. A user finding an interesting visualisation can immediately understand what data shape is required, copy the manifest, adapt the column names, and render their own version.

### Gallery Generation

The `bulk_debug_viz_factory_layers.py` runner will support a `--gallery` flag that auto-generates a `gallery_report.md` document combining all three recipe parts for every registered component.

```bash
# Generate full gallery across all VizFactory layers:
./.venv/bin/python libs/viz_factory/tests/bulk_debug_viz_factory_layers.py \
    --gallery \
    --output tmp/gallery
```

---

## 4. The Premiere Entry: Figshare AMR Triple-Source Integration

The flagship gallery entry is the **Phase 9 Triple-Source Integration**, which provides the most complete end-to-end demonstration of the platform's capabilities.

### Data Recipe

| Source | File | Shape | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `fg_metadata` (Anchor) | `fg_metadata.tsv` | 991 × 6 | E. coli isolates (cat/dog/horse/human) |
| `fg_phenotypes` | `fg_phenotypes.tsv` | 18,438 × 4 | MIC values for 19 antibiotics per isolate |
| `fg_genotypes` | `fg_genotypes.tsv` | 10,840 × 7 | Resistance genes and AMR class annotations |
| **Joined Frame** | `figshare_join_check.tsv` | **203,957 × 10** | Full long-format tidy table |

> Reference: Chung et al. (2023), Figshare DOI: [10.6084/m9.figshare.21737288.v1](https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.21737288.v1), CC-BY 4.0

### Manifest Recipe

```yaml
# Assembly manifest: config/manifests/pipelines/figshare_integration.yaml
assembly_manifests:
  - anchor: fg_metadata
    join:
      - source: fg_phenotypes
        on: sample_id
        how: left
      - source: fg_genotypes
        on: sample_id
        how: left
        pre_drop: [host_animal_common]  # Collision guard
```

### Plot Recipes (Stage 3)

Three integration plots are pre-built as gallery entries:

| Plot | Manifest Key | Insight |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **A** — Phenotype per Antibiotic | `plot_A_phenotype_per_antibiotic` | R/I/S distribution across 19 antibiotics |
| **B** — Gene Family per Host | `plot_B_gene_family_per_host` | Top 12 AMR gene families by host animal |
| **C** — Resistance Class per Host | `plot_C_resistance_class_per_host` | AMR class burden across host species |

See `assets/scripts/figshare_plot_integration.py` for the plot script.

---

## 5. The Platform Roadmap (Next Sprint)

The shift from tool to platform requires a short, focused sprint:

| Priority | Task | Deliverable |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **High** | Implement `--gallery` flag in `bulk_debug_viz_factory_layers.py` | `tmp/gallery/gallery_report.md` |
| **High** | Add gallery entry for Phase 9 plots (manually) | Figshare AMR gallery page |
| **Medium** | Quarto gallery page: `docs/vision/gallery.qmd` | Browsable cookbook |
| **Low** | Interactive manifest editor (future) | Shiny UI YAML text area |

---

## 6. Connection to the Technical Appendix

For the full architectural rationale behind the design choices in this chapter, see:

- `VizFactory (viz_factory.py)` architecture: [Viz Factory Rationale](../appendix/viz_factory_rationale.qmd)
- Data flow and assembly: [Wrangling & Assembly](../workflows/wrangling.qmd)
- ADR-025 Gallery & Recipe Pattern: [Architecture Decisions](../../.antigravity/knowledge/architecture_decisions.md)
